text.title: Domain Health Check and RAG Assessment
text.summary: RAG status assessment procedure, domain-owner RACI pattern, cross-domain interaction chains, and agent gotchas for PMBOK 8 domain health checks.

rule.statement: Anchor every domain color rating with a quoted artifact (retro line, incident, missed milestone); ratings without evidence are not valid inputs.
rule.rationale: Unanchored RAG ratings default to Green due to politeness bias; evidence quotes force the assessor to confront actual data.

rule.statement: Re-score domain RAG monthly minimum AND on triggers: incident, scope change, leadership change.
rule.rationale: The value of the framework is the trend line, not the snapshot; trigger-based re-scoring catches mid-cycle regressions that monthly cadence misses.

list.item: Green: domain performing as expected, no gaps requiring action
Amber: domain has a documented gap with an active remediation plan
Red: domain has a critical gap with no remediation plan, or remediation failing

rule.statement: Assign one Accountable owner per domain; one person can hold multiple domains, but no domain can be unowned.
rule.rationale: Domains with no named owner drift to Green in status reports regardless of actual health; accountability produces honest reporting.

list.item: Risk → PM with risk register owner

section.title: Cross-Domain Interaction Chains
section.p: Domains are interdependent; a Red in one domain typically cascades to Amber or Red in others. Common chains:

list.item: Resources Red → Schedule Amber (capacity gap delays delivery)
Stakeholders Red → Finance Amber (sponsor disengagement delays approvals)
Governance Red → Scope Amber (unclear decision rights cause scope creep)
Risk Red → all domains (unmitigated risk materializes everywhere simultaneously)

rule.statement: When multiple domains are Red, identify which single domain improvement unblocks the most others; fix that first.
rule.rationale: Parallel remediation across all Red domains spreads PM attention too thin and produces no completed fixes; sequenced priority maximizes recovery velocity.

antipattern.description: Agent rates every domain Green when not given calibration examples.
antipattern.reason: Politeness bias in LLM outputs; provide one explicit Red exemplar in the prompt to force calibration.

antipattern.description: Agent conflates PMBOK 7 vocabulary (Performance Domains) with PMBOK 8 vocabulary (seven domains) in the same output.
antipattern.reason: PMBOK 7 has eight domains; PMBOK 8 has seven with different names. Pin the version in the system prompt to prevent cross-contamination.

antipattern.description: Long corpora cause domain conflation — one agent inference for all domains simultaneously.
antipattern.reason: Chunk by domain (one inference per domain) for higher signal; combined inference loses domain-specific evidence in the noise.

text.reference: Per-project domain status manifest with owner, evidence, and gap fields
